content_en:
Ba-Fadl al-Hadhrami's treatment of salah in Al-Muqaddimah presents the essential Shafi'i prayer law in a format suited to the beginning student. He begins with the categories of prayer — obligatory (fard 'ayn on every Muslim, such as the five daily prayers), obligatory by collective sufficiency (fard kifayah, such as the funeral prayer), and recommended (sunnah).

The five daily prayers — Fajr (two rak'ahs), Dhuhr (four), 'Asr (four), Maghrib (three), and 'Isha (four) — are established by the Quran, Sunnah, and the consensus of the entire Muslim community. Their times, conditions, and method are presented with the clarity that makes Al-Muqaddimah ideal for the beginning student and for non-Arabic-speaking populations where the text has been widely translated and taught.

The conditions of prayer are listed: Islam, discernment (tamyiz), removal of ritual impurity (hadath), removal of physical impurity (najasah) from body, clothing, and place of prayer, covering the 'awrah, facing the qiblah, and entering the prayer time. Ba-Fadl's concise presentation of these conditions allows students to quickly internalize them before proceeding to the more detailed rulings in advanced texts.

The pillars of prayer are presented as seventeen, following the standard Shafi'i enumeration. Ba-Fadl notes the importance of each pillar and the consequence of omitting one — the prayer is invalid and must be repeated. His treatment is brief here, relying on the teacher's oral explanation and the commentaries to fill in the details.

The obligatory acts (wajibat) that are not pillars — primarily the tashahhud positions and their related recitations — are mentioned as items whose forgetful omission requires sujud as-sahw (prostration of forgetfulness). The Shafi'i school performs sujud as-sahw after the salam, not before it as the Hanafi school requires.

The sunan of prayer are many and contribute to the completeness and spiritual quality of the prayer. Ba-Fadl lists the key ones: the opening supplication (du'a al-istiftah), seeking refuge in Allah from Shaytan (ta'awwudh), reciting a surah after al-Fatihah in the first two rak'ahs, the takbirat of transition, the tasbih in ruku' and sujud beyond the minimum once, and the du'a at the end before salam.

Ba-Fadl closes the prayer chapter with a reminder that the prayer is the pillar of the religion (imad ad-din) and the distinguishing mark of the believer. The Prophet (peace be upon him) said: 'Between a man and shirk and kufr is the abandonment of prayer' (Muslim). The obligation of prayer is more certain than any other ruling in Islam, and its performance is the surest sign of a Muslim's commitment to their Creator. No matter how pressed by worldly affairs, the Muslim never abandons the five daily prayers.
content_id:
Pembahasan Ba-Fadhl al-Hadrami tentang salat dalam Al-Muqaddimah menyajikan hukum salat Syafi'i yang esensial dalam format yang sesuai untuk pelajar pemula. Beliau dimulai dengan kategori-kategori salat — fardhu 'ain (wajib bagi setiap Muslim, seperti lima salat harian), fardhu kifayah (wajib kolektif, seperti salat jenazah), sunnah mu'akkadah (sangat dianjurkan, seperti salat witir dan rawatib), dan sunnah ghairu mu'akkadah (dianjurkan namun tidak setinggi yang sebelumnya).

Syarat-syarat sah salat mencakup masuk waktu, bersuci dari hadats dan najis, menutup aurat, menghadap kiblat, dan niat. Setiap syarat dipaparkan dengan ringkas namun cukup untuk memberikan pemahaman yang memadai. Posisi Syafi'i tentang beberapa syarat — seperti keharusan menutup aurat secara sempurna (tidak hanya sekadar) dan tentang penentuan arah kiblat — disampaikan dengan jelas.

Rukun-rukun salat dalam mazhab Syafi'i berjumlah tujuh belas, yang merupakan jumlah yang lebih banyak dari mazhab lain karena memasukkan beberapa hal yang mazhab lain anggap sebagai sunnah. Ba-Fadhl menyebutkan setiap rukun dengan ringkas: niat, takbiratul ihram, berdiri bagi yang mampu, membaca Al-Fatihah, ruku', tuma'ninah dalam ruku', bangkit dari ruku', tuma'ninah dalam i'tidal, sujud dua kali, tuma'ninah dalam sujud, duduk antara dua sujud, tuma'ninah dalam duduk, tasyahhud akhir, membaca shalawat dalam tasyahhud akhir, duduk ketika tasyahhud akhir, salam pertama, dan tertib.

Penyebutan tuma'ninah (ketenangan) sebagai rukun tersendiri adalah ciri khas mazhab Syafi'i yang menekankan pentingnya kekhusyukan dan ketenangan dalam setiap gerakan salat. Pembahasan ini ditutup dengan hal-hal yang membatalkan salat, yang disajikan secara ringkas dan mudah diingat.
